lines: Paddy! Grand to see ye at the footrace. The Brian P. Kelly Memorial — started in his honor back in 1978.
Brian was a beloved Scranton man — ran the parade every year, rain or shine.
We've got a spare race bib for ye — number one, no less! — but first, prove you know your Irish running history!
Well run, Paddy! That green bib is yours — wear it proudly on the float!
Now get yourself to St. Peter's — Father Callahan is expecting ye!
The bib is yours, Paddy! Go get it and head up to the Cathedral!
Top o' the mornin', Paddy! Oh, I'm so glad ye're here!
The St. Patrick's Parade starts at noon — but we're in terrible trouble!
Grand Marshal O'Brien says we're missing FIVE essential items for the parade!
Without them, the parade can't begin. Scranton would be heartbroken!
We need: a Shamrock Bouquet, the Parade Banner, a Lucky Horseshoe, a Green Light Bulb — and a Race Bib from the Kelly Memorial Footrace!
Before ye go, Paddy — I've a quick quiz for ye. Show everyone ye know your Irish history!
Brilliant! Now, there's a Shamrock Bouquet right there on the table — take it!
Then pop over to the Kelly Footrace, St. Peter's Cathedral, Steamtown, and Lackawanna Avenue.
Hurry, Paddy! The clock is ticking! Erin go Bragh!
Ye have everything from here, Paddy! Now go — the parade needs ye!
Ah, Paddy! God bless ye! Are ye here about the parade?
We have Parade Day Mass at 11 o'clock sharp — always have. The whole parish turns out before joining the march!
The Irish-Catholic community has marched in this parade for over 150 years.
Our parish came here fleeing the Great Famine of 1845 to 1852. Scranton gave us hope.
I have the Parade Banner right here — but first, let's see if ye know your Irish history!
Well done! The Parade Banner is over there by the wall — go ahead and take it.
God bless ye, Paddy. May the road rise up to meet ye!
May God be with ye, Paddy! Take the banner and go!
Ho there, young Paddy! Here to see the old steam engines?
My grandfather came from County Mayo during the Famine. Worked the coal mines here.
Then his son — my father — worked the railroads. Irish hands built this country, they did!
Tens of thousands of Irish immigrants came to the Wyoming Valley. Coal and steel, that was Scranton.
Before I give ye what ye need, let's see if ye know the story!
Sharp as a tack! That Lucky Horseshoe on the fence — it's yours for the parade.
Open end up, now! That's how ye keep the luck inside!
Give 'em a good parade, Paddy. Make the old Irish proud!
Go on, Paddy! Take that horseshoe and march on!
Paddy! Thank goodness! We've been looking for someone to help!
The Electric City sign is lit up green just for the parade — isn't it gorgeous?
Oh! I just remembered — someone left a Green Light Bulb on the sidewalk over there.
We need it to light the parade route! But first — parade trivia time!
Wonderful! The green light bulb is right over there — grab it quick!
Then head to Mulberry and Wyoming to find Grand Marshal O'Brien.
You're almost there, Paddy! The parade is counting on ye!
You've got everything! Go find the Grand Marshal — hurry!
Paddy, lad! I've been waiting for ye!
The parade cannot begin without those five items.
Every St. Patrick's Parade in Scranton tells our story — the story of Irish immigrants.
They came with nothing but their faith and their courage. And they built this city.
Do ye have everything? Let's find out with one last bit of trivia!
All five items! The Shamrock Bouquet, the Parade Banner, the Lucky Horseshoe, the Green Light Bulb — and the Race Bib from this morning's footrace!
Scranton, are ye ready?! LET THE PARADE BEGIN!!!
ERIN GO BRAGH!
Hmm, we're still missing some items, Paddy.
Check your inventory — you'll need all five before we can start!
The Shamrock Bouquet, Parade Banner, Lucky Horseshoe, Green Light Bulb, and Race Bib.
